web_results.title: Fed Leaves Rates Unchanged to Start 2026: Is a Cut Coming in March? | J.P. Morgan
web_results.description: The Federal Reserve (Fed) held ... sees low odds of the Fed choosing to cut at its March meeting, but our strategists still expect one rate cut in 2026....

web_results.title: Will the Fed Cut Interest Rates in 2026? Don't Get Your Hopes Up, Some Experts Say
web_results.description: Feroli's prediction goes against the expectation of financial market participants, who are pricing in a likelihood of two quarter-point cuts in the Fed's benchmark rate in 2026, according to the CME Group's FedWatch tool, which forecasts rate movements based on fed funds futures trading data. In their latest quarterly projections last month, the Fed's seven governors and the presidents of the 12 regional Fed banks penciled in an expectation on average of a single quarter-point cut this year, which would bring the key federal funds rate to a range of 3.25% to 3.5%. At the time, eight projected no more than two quarter-point cuts in 2026, while four called for more-aggressive reductions, and seven were leaning against any adjustments.

web_results.title: Fed Outlook 2026: Rate Forecasts and Fixed Income Strategies | iShares
web_results.description: ... At its final meeting of 2025, the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points to a range of 3.50% to 3.75%; the Fed has now cut rates by 175 basis points since September 2024.2

news_results.description: Goolsbee says the Fed won’t cut interest rates until inflation moves closer to the 2% target.

news_results.title: Fed's Goolsbee: Rate cuts appropriate if inflation falls, but too soon to bet on productivity | Reuters
news_results.description: The U.S. Federal Reserve could resume cutting interest rates if inflation starts to fall, but it would be risky to use expected productivity growth as a reason to loosen monetary policy now, Chicago Fed president Austan Goolsbee said as he waded into what is emerging as a core debate at the ...
